cot_prefix: Okay, let's take a look at the question. The question is asking what we know about garage sales based on the provided text. The options are A, B, C, and D. First, let me recall the relevant part of the text. The paragraph about garage sales says: "Perhaps even more enjoyable than flea markets are the weekend outdoor garage sales which have now become very much a part of American culture. These are held usually in the spring and fall when the weather is still good." So right away, I can see that garage sales are part of American culture, which might relate to option C. The text also mentions that garage sales are held "usually in the spring and fall," which means they're not only in the spring, so option A is incorrect. Then it says, "More than likely the goods on display are in good condition and great buys can be found if one has the time and patience to search carefully." This contradicts option B, which claims the goods are totally useless. So B is wrong. Regarding option D: "If the owner is in a position where he must sell his home or plans to move soon, then he may be forced to practically give things away at a price far below their true worth." This suggests that some items might be sold below their value, but not all. The question says "All the goods are selling far below their real value," which is too absolute.
